free_text: Small-Angles Scattering of 143 MeV Polarized Protons.
Consisted  of  Three  0.16  Cm  Thick  Pilot  B Plastic
Counter  1 was 2.86 Cm Wide and 7.62 Cm High, Counter 2
Was  2.86*8.89  Cm.   The  Defining   Counter   3   was
2.54*10.16  Cm  and  was 3.169 Meter from the Centre of
The Target.  The Total-Energy Counter 4  was  6.35*15.2
Cm  and was 6.35 Cm Thick, It was 7.6 Cm Behind Counter
3.  Since Counter 4 was Capable of Completely  Stopping
Only  Protons  of  Less  Than 90 MeV, a Copper Absorber
(13.5 G/CM**2) Was Placed Just in Front of Counter  4.
The  Total-Energy  Crystal  had an Energy Resolution of
Photomultipliers  Used  in  the  Telescope were All Rca
All Energies are at Target Centers and are
Data Errors are Due to Statistics and Possible
Misalignments. The Cross Sections were Measured with a
Relative Error of About 0.7% and Absolute Uncertainties
Of 3% and Error 4% on the Polarizations Exclusive of
Effects Arising from Multiple Scattering. All Results
Are in the Centre-Of-Mass System of Coordinates.
99.2% Purity was Used.  Principal Impurity in Beryllium
Was Used. Influence of Ta-180 is Very Low.
